definition: The current annual recurring revenue for an enterprise account, cast from current_enterprise_arr on the enterprise company record.
new users created who have a signup date, excluding builder.io domain emails
distinct visitor ids
subscriptions from stripe with plan = ‘self-serve’
Week-over-week growth rate of signups: (Current Week - Previous Week) ÷ Previous Week. Available by individual channel or across all channels combined.
table: Computed via LAG window function over weekly signup counts

definition: Per-URL and per-Referrer performance pivot showing: New Visitors, Signups, % Signups, SS Paid Subs, % Paid Subs, Marketing Contacts, % ICP Signups.
Count of users taken on the last day of each month for an account. Displayed as a line chart trending over time.
Percentage of signups that convert to at least one paid subscription. Calculated as Paid Subscribers ÷ Total Signups.

Percentage of an account's users who had at least one session in the trailing 30-day window. Calculated as countdistinct(users with sessions) / total users. Sessions filtered by session_raw_date within range.
Signups ÷ New Visitors for a given time period and channel. Measures conversion rate from first visit to account creation.

Total deal amount for deals at the S1/Qualified stage. qualified_pipeline_flag = Yes when stage is NOT IN ('Automated Deal', 'Meeting Disqualified', 'S0 - Unqualified', 'S0 - MIAM'). Excludes Self-Serve pipelines.
Number of active spaces (num_spaces) divided by contracted_spaces. Shows how much of the contracted space allocation the account is using.
Contacts who have moved from S0 stage (date_moved_from_s0 is not null). Cohorted by date_moved_from_s0.
Comma-separated list of distinct product names purchased by the account (e.g. Publish, Fusion). Derived from listaggdistinct of the products field from HubSpot deals, ordered alphabetically.
Current month's total pageviews divided by contracted_pageviews. Includes MoM comparison calculated as current_month_pageviews / previous_month_pageviews.
Boolean flag indicating whether a user is on at least one Paid Space. Used as a filter to isolate users who have converted to paid subscriptions.
Total deal amount at the pre-qualified stage — deals that exist but haven't yet been qualified by sales. Excludes disqualified deals.
A contact where the AE has committed to pursuing the contact. Tracked by date_entered_sal in HubSpot contacts.

Count of distinct active_user values per week (truncated to Monday). An active user has a page view on the web app or generated an AI chat. Excludes Shopify orgs (kind <> 'shopify'), test orgs, and internal subscriptions (subscription_name <> 'internal').
Customer success health classification for an account. Values: healthy, On the Radar, or contains Churn. Sourced from the health_status field on the enterprise company.
Current month's total bandwidth usage divided by contracted_bandwidth. Includes MoM comparison. Excludes internal subscriptions — same filter logic as pageviews.
Signups where Company_Fit_Score ≥ 4
queryTemplate:
SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC(signup_date, DAY) as date,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN Company_Fit_Score >= 4 THEN user_id END) as icp_signups,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as total_signups,
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN Company_Fit_Score >= 4 THEN user_id END) / COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) * 100, 1) as icp_percentage
FROM dbt_analytics.hs_contacts
WHERE signup_date BETWEEN @start_date AND @end_date
  AND email NOT LIKE '%@builder.io'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC
commonQuestions:
How many high-quality signups did we get this week?
What's our ICP conversion rate compared to last month?
Which channels are driving the most ICP signups?
Are we hitting our 2x ICP signup growth goal?
knownGotchas:
Company_Fit_Score can be NULL for old contacts (before 2025-01-15)
Always exclude @builder.io emails to avoid counting internal tests
Signup date is based on first form submission, not user creation
Score threshold of 4 is the current ICP definition but may change quarterly
joinPattern:
-- Join to get organization details
FROM dbt_analytics.hs_contacts hc
LEFT JOIN dbt_analytics.dim_organizations org
  ON hc.organization_id = org.id

-- Join to exclude test/internal accounts
WHERE hc.email NOT LIKE '%@builder.io'
  AND hc.is_deleted = FALSE
exampleUseCase: Marketing wants to know if the new enterprise campaign increased high-quality signups. Query last 30 days of ICP signups, segment by utm_campaign, and compare week-over-week growth to identify which campaigns are working.

definition: A percentage score representing the health of a customer's Publish product usage. Stored as product_health_pct, filtered by product type. Scoring methodology linked in Notion.
The next renewal date for the account's contract, stored as upcoming_renewal_date on the enterprise company record.
If root_org_id is null or empty, fall back to org_id. This logic is repeated across nearly every query and is a core concept for org-level aggregation.
Ratio of current active users to contracted_user_seats. Represents how much of the seat allocation the customer is consuming.
Foundational filter applied across nearly all queries: subscription_name NOT IN ('free', 'internal') AND kind <> 'shopify' AND org name does not contain 'test'.
Count of distinct first-time visitors (by visitor_id) cohorted by their first pageview date. Only counts users whose first-ever pageview falls within the selected date range.
queryTemplate:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC(first_pageview_date, DAY) as cohort_date,
  COUNT(DISTINCT visitor_id) as new_visitors
FROM dbt_staging_bigquery.first_pageviews
WHERE first_pageview_date BETWEEN @start_date AND @end_date
  AND is_internal_traffic = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC
commonQuestions:
How many new visitors did we get today?
What's the week-over-week growth in traffic?
Which landing pages are attracting the most new visitors?
How does organic vs paid traffic compare for new visitor acquisition?
knownGotchas:
Excludes internal traffic (Builder.io IPs and @builder.io emails)
Visitor_id can be unstable if users clear cookies
First pageview is determined by earliest timestamp in Amplitude, not session start
Cross-device visitors may appear as multiple new visitors
joinPattern:
-- Join with pageviews for channel attribution
FROM dbt_staging_bigquery.first_pageviews fp
LEFT JOIN dbt_staging_bigquery.pageviews pv
  ON fp.visitor_id = pv.visitor_id
  AND fp.first_pageview_date = pv.pageview_date
exampleUseCase: Product marketing wants to measure the impact of the new homepage design. Compare new visitors before/after launch date, segment by traffic source, and calculate conversion rate to signup for each segment.

definition: ARR from self-serve paid subscriptions, measured per user to track back to pageview. First-touch pageview must occur before the subscription start date (v.created_date < sub.start_date).

Count of distinct users who created an account, cohorted by User_Create_D. Pageview Centric view excludes signups without a tracked pageview (e.g. adblock/Figma plugin). Signup Centric view includes ALL product signups.
The close_date from the earliest HubSpot deal associated with the account's root org. Represents when the customer first became a paying customer.
Average number of sessions per user per month. Calculated by counting session_id per user per month (truncated to month), then averaging across all users. Filtered to the current billing period.

Attributed acquisition channel based on a user's first tracked touchpoint. Coalesce_Channel prioritizes First_Touch_Channel over the pageview-level Channel field. Values: direct, organic, figma, oss, ai chat, paid, referral, social, other.

metric: Contracted Seats / Bandwidth / Pageviews / AI Credits
definition: Contractual limits for an account across key resource dimensions: contracted_user_seats, contracted_bandwidth, contracted_pageviews, contracted_ai_credits. These serve as the denominators for all utilization % metrics.
table: dbt_mart.dim_organizations (paid orgs query, subscription_name NOT IN ('free', 'internal'))

definition: QLs ÷ Signups for a given time period and channel. Measures conversion rate from signup to qualified lead status.
table: Computed from QL and Signup metrics

definition: If root_org_id is null or empty, fall back to org_id. This logic is repeated across nearly every query and is a core concept for org-level aggregation.
Foundational filter applied across nearly all queries: subscription_name NOT IN ('free', 'internal') AND kind <> 'shopify' AND org name does not contain 'test'.

Total deal amount for qualified open deals (S1, S2, S3 stages). Excludes S0 (pre-qualified) and closed deals (Closed Won, Closed Lost). Cohorted by first pageview date when used in acquisition context.
